segments.text: TIM: Hi, Laura - could you spare a few minutes to talk about the work placement you did last summer?
segments.translation: 提姆：嗨，萝拉。你可以花几分钟跟我说一下去年夏天你做的工作实习吗？

segments.text: I'm thinking of doing one myself ...
segments.translation: 我自己也在考虑去实习。

segments.text: LAURA: Hi, Tim. Sure.
segments.translation: 萝拉：嗨，提姆。当然可以。

segments.text: TIM: Didn't you do yours at an environmental services company?
segments.translation: 提姆：你是不是在一个环境服务公司做的？

segments.text: LAURA: That's right ... It's only a very small company and they needed someone to produce a company brochure, and I wanted to get some business experience because I'm interested in a career in occupational psychology in a business environment.
segments.translation: 萝拉：是的…… 它是一个很小的公司，需要有人帮他们生产公司手册。因为我对于商业环境下职业心理学相关的职业感兴趣，所以我想有些行业经验。

segments.text: It was good because I had overall responsibility for the project.
segments.translation: 这工作不错，因为我可以全权负责整个项目。

segments.text: TIM: What kind of skills do you think you developed on the placement?
segments.translation: 提姆：在实习中你觉得你自己锻炼了什么技能？

segments.text: I mean, apart from the ones you already had ...
segments.translation: 我的意思是，除了你现有的以外。

segments.text: Did you have to do all the artwork for the brochure, the layout and everything?
segments.translation: 你需要做所有手册的美工吗，排版什么的？

segments.text: LAURA: We hired the services of a professional photographer for that.
segments.translation: 萝拉：我们雇了一个职业摄影师来做这些。

segments.text: I did have to use my IT skills to a certain extent because I cut and pasted text from marketing leaflets, but that didn't involve anything I hadn't done before.
segments.translation: 从某种程度来讲，我的确得用到我的IT技术，因为我需要从市场传单中剪切和粘贴文本，但是这些都是我之前做过的事情。

segments.text: TIM: Do you think you got any better at managing your time and prioritising things?
segments.translation: 提姆：你觉得你自己在管理时间和优先处理事情方面有没有长进？

segments.text: You always used to say you had trouble with that ...
segments.translation: 你原来总说在这些方面你总遇到些问题……

segments.text: LAURA: Oh, definitely. There was so much pressure to meet the project deadline.
segments.translation: 萝拉：哦当然。在赶项目的截止日期时总是有很大的压力。

segments.text: And I also got better at explaining things and asserting my opinions, because I had to have weekly consultations with the marketing manager and give him a progress report.
segments.translation: 我解释说明，表达观点的能力也更好了，因为我必须每周和市场经理进行商讨，并且和他汇报进展。

segments.text: TIM: It sounds as if you got a lot out of it then.
segments.translation: 提姆：听起来你从中收获很多啊。

segments.text: LAURA: Absolutely. It was really worthwhile …
segments.translation: 萝拉：必须的。这真的很值得……

segments.text: But you know, the company benefited too ...
segments.translation: 但是你知道，公司也从中获益。

segments.text: TIM: Yes, they must have done.
segments.translation: 提姆：是的，他们当然如此。

segments.text: After all, if they'd used a professional advertising agency to produce their brochure instead of doing it in-house, presumably they'd have paid a lot more?
segments.translation: 毕竟，如果他们让专业的广告代理来制作手册，而不是在公司内部完成，估计他们要多花很多钱？

segments.text: LAURA: Oh, yes. I worked it out - it would have been 250 per cent more.
segments.translation: 萝拉：哦是的。我算下来，要多花超过250%。

segments.text: And I thought the end result was good, even though we did everything on site.
segments.translation: 我想最终的结果不错，尽管我们每件事都是在公司里完成的。

segments.text: The company has quite a powerful computer and I managed to borrow some scanning software from the university.
segments.translation: 公司的计算机很强大，我设法从学校借了一些扫描软件。

segments.text: The new brochure looks really professional; it enhances the image of the company straight away.
segments.translation: 新的手册看起来非常专业，立刻提升了公司的形象。

segments.text: TIM: So in the long run it should help them to attract clients, and improve their sales figures?
segments.translation: 提姆：所以从长远来看，这可以帮他们吸引客户，并且提升他们的销售数据？

segments.text: LAURA: That's the idea. Yeah.
segments.translation: 萝拉：就是这个意思。是的。

segments.text: TIM: Well, all in all it sounds very positive - I think I will go ahead and apply for a placement myself.
segments.translation: 提姆：那么，总体来说听起来很积极。我想我要继续申请我自己的实习了。

segments.text: How do I go about it?
segments.translation: 我怎么样做呢？

segments.text: LAURA: It's easy enough to do, because there's a government agency called STEP - S-T-E-P - that organises placements for students.
segments.translation: 萝拉：其实很简单，因为有个叫做“STEP”S-T-E-P的政府机构为学生组织实习。

segments.text: You should start by getting their booklet with all the details - I expect you can download one from their website.
segments.translation: 你应当在开始的时候拿他们的手册看看所有具体的信息。我估计你可以从网上下载。

segments.text: TIM: Actually, they've got copies in the psychology department - I've seen them there.
segments.translation: 提姆：事实上，心理学系有几份，我在那儿见过。

segments.text: I'll just go to the office and pick one up.
segments.translation: 我要赶紧去办公室拿一份。

segments.text: LAURA: Right. And then if I were you, after I'd looked at it I'd go over all the options with someone …
segments.translation: 萝拉：好的。然后如果我是你，看完后我会找个人分析所有的选项。

segments.text: TIM: I suppose I should ask my tutor's advice.
segments.translation: 提姆：我想我应该让我的导师给些建议。

segments.text: He knows more about me than anyone.
segments.translation: 他比任何人都了解我。

segments.text: LAURA: One of the career officers would be better.
segments.translation: 萝拉：找一个职业顾问应该更好些。

segments.text: They've got more knowledge about the jobs market than your personal tutor would have.
segments.translation: 他们比你的个人导师更了解就业市场。

segments.text: TIM: OK ...
segments.translation: 提姆：好的……

segments.text: LAURA: And then when you know what you want you can register with STEP -you'll find their address in the booklet.
segments.translation: 萝拉：然后，当你知道自己想和STEP在哪方面进行登记后，你能在手册上找到它的地址。

segments.text: And once you've registered they assign you to a mentor who looks after your application.
segments.translation: 一旦注册成功，他们会给你分配一位负责你的申请的导师。

segments.text: TIM: And then I suppose you just sit back and wait till you hear something?
segments.translation: 提姆：然后我估计你就坐等接下来的消息？

segments.text: LAURA: They told me at the careers office that it's best to be proactive, and get updates yourself by checking the website for new placement alerts.
segments.translation: 萝拉：职业办公室的人告诉我最好要积极主动些，自己通过查找网页的新实习提醒去更新你所掌握的情况。

segments.text: Your mentor is supposed to keep you informed, but you can't rely on that.
segments.translation: 你的导师应该会告知你这些信息，但是你不能依靠这种方式。

segments.text: TIM: I don't suppose it's a good idea to get in touch with companies directly, is it?
segments.translation: 提姆：我觉得不应该直接和公司联系，是不是？

segments.text: LAURA: Not really... But it is the company who notifies you if they want you to go for an interview.
segments.translation: 萝拉：不太好。但是的确是公司通知你是不是需要你去参加面试。

segments.text: You get a letter of invitation or an email from Personnel departments.
segments.translation: 你会从人事部门收到一封邀请信或者电邮。

segments.text: TIM: And do I reply directly to them?
segments.translation: 提姆：我要直接回复他们吗？

segments.text: LAURA: Yes, you do.
segments.translation: 萝拉：是的。

segments.text: STEP only gets involved again once you've been made a job offer.
segments.translation: 只有在你拿到录取通知后STEP会再次参与其中。

segments.text: TIM: Right ... So, once you've had an interview you should let your mentor know what the outcome is?
segments.translation: 提姆：好的。所以，一旦参加了面试，你应该让导师知道你的结果？

segments.text: I mean whether you're offered a job, and whether you've decided to accept it?
segments.translation: 我的意思是不管你有没有得到这份工作，也不论你决没决定接受这个份工作？

segments.text: LAURA: That's right.
segments.translation: 萝拉：是的。

segments.text: They'll inform the careers office once a placement has been agreed, so you don't have to do that.
segments.translation: 一旦确认实习，他们会通知职业办公室。所以你不必这么做。

segments.text: TIM: Is that all then?
segments.translation: 提姆：就这些了吗？

segments.text: LAURA: More or less.
segments.translation: 萝拉：差不多吧。

segments.text: Only once you've accepted an offer you'll probably have to supply a reference, because the placement will be conditional on that.
segments.translation: 只有当你接受入职通知，你可能要提供一封推荐信，因为实习的条件是基于这个的。

segments.text: And that's something you should ask your own tutor to provide.
segments.translation: 这是你需要让你的个人导师提供的。

segments.text: He knows about your academic ability and also about your qualities, like reliability.
segments.translation: 他知道你的学习能力和你的素质，比如可靠度。

segments.text: TIM: Well, thanks very much for the information - I'm starting to look forward ...
segments.translation: 提姆：好的，非常感谢你的信息，我开始期待了……
